Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and strengthens mathematical modeling and problem-solving skills for employment (Target 4.4). Both models correctly identified this.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course's focus on finding 'optimal solutions' for decisions directly supports higher economic productivity and resource efficiency (Targets 8.2, 8.4). Model B's original reasoning was more comprehensive.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course explicitly covers models for 'routing,' 'queuing,' and 'warehousing,' which are fundamental for resilient infrastructure and industrial processes (Targets 9.1, 9.4). Model B's original reasoning was stronger.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: Course topics like 'routing' and 'queuing' are fundamental for planning sustainable transport systems and managing urban services (Targets 11.2, 11.3). Model B's original reasoning was stronger.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course's goal of finding the 'optimal solution' and managing 'warehousing' directly supports efficient resource use and waste reduction (Targets 12.2, 12.5). Model B's original reasoning was stronger.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The study of 'Game Theory' and 'Decision Making' provides analytical tools for understanding strategic interactions, applicable to developing effective institutions (Targets 16.6, 16.7). Model B's original reasoning was stronger.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course builds capacity in 'data collection and analysis' and 'scientific computing,' supporting measurements for sustainable development progress (Targets 17.18, 17.19). Model B's original reasoning was stronger.
